review_title: Very useful when you want to host your scapers
author: Rami M.
pros: Did the Job I wanted it to do for me.... Which is host and test your scraper that runs in the background. Before Zyte, I had to run the script inside my terminal and that killed my battery
cons: Getting started wasn't as smooth as I'm used to with services like Vercel. The user experience using vercel makes you feel like you are using a premium, top tier service. I didn't feel that when I used Zyte. Part of the reason is the UI, but there are a lot of other aspects like the snappiness and speed of the website. It took quite some time to test out my implementation. I would need to wait for some time for the build to complete to try running the web scraper again
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Best data extraction & automated proxy service
description: We chose Zyte after testing all leading proxy and data extraction solutions on the web. The service is extremely reliable, rapidly scalable and has the lowest latency amongst its peers. Support teams are extremely responsive even on weekends and we've never been let down.
author: Tushar S.
pros: - Reliable & consistent (highest uptime) - Lowest latency - Lowest resolution errors - Excellent customer support
cons: - Pricing can be improved - IP availability in non-US locations can be improved

review_title: no way to control your spending
author: John F.
pros: wasting my money and getting ripped off by zyte
cons: They advertise a feature to set a spending limit, but there is no option. I contacted support, sent a screenshot, they said there is no spending limit without a subscription. The entire system is designed to steal your money. I woke up yesterday to a bill 40x times more than what was expected. Support is unhelpful and they don't care, they just want to steal your money. Zyte is designed to be misleading.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It makes me work really fast but still secure for my clients. I look really professional even though what I did was only suggesting and installing Crawlera. It's awesome!
author: Aminah N.
pros: It really saves my time as a web scraping specialists. I always need rotated proxies for my client needs. I keep creating a new project and it is a lot of hassle of I have to buy proxies, renew proxies, and create proxy rotation on my own. I am glad I don't even need to think about it. It just works!
cons: Well, it's just doesn't have enough advanced documentation on Scrapy Splash combined with Crawlera. Their development team in Github is very responsive though.
author_profession: Web Scraping Specialist & PHP Web Developer

description: Our company, whatoplay.com, aggregates data from multiple sources around the world. Since we started using Crawlera, we haven't really encountered big issues. Our common ones in the last 6 months is just reaching our limit, so we just had to increase our plan.
author: Neil Joseph Y.
pros: We're only using Crawlera for our data scraping needs and it has worked seamlessly since we started using it in 2016. When we had to upgrade to a higher limit, the transition was fast, and no dev time was wasted updating our existing codebase.
cons: Pricing can be a bit tricky especially if your need is right in between the plans.

description: I have a good experience with scrappinhumb, in a matter of minutes I can develop and publish a new spider.
author: Jose Iram D.
pros: The software delivers the exactly promised result. It has a friendly interface and an efficient support.
cons: You need a roadmap of new features and the pricing of third-party services.

review_title: Amazing product - can't get any better for speedy data mining
description: Overall experience with them has been stellar, I will likely be a customer for life.
author: 🤖 Curtis B.
pros: I like scalability. Sometimes we need to submit millions of requests immediately and with Scrapinghub we can really put the pedal to the metal. We rarely have downtime, and it's really allowed us to grow our company and thrive. Easy to integrate into scrapy and other python tools.
cons: Their billing support can be moody, but the product is fantastic!!

review_title: Why do you need that title?
description: I use and recommend that platform for years for my customers which need production-ready enterprise-grade data scraping systems.
author: Mikhail K.
pros: - Original and flexible technology - No vendor-lock - Easy to use for professionals - Pretty convenient API system for integration with third-party solutions
cons: - Not so easy to use by non-professional IT person that still wants to use data scraping - Lack of ability to create some kind of simple and clear user interface for such a persons - No simple solution for distributed/high-volume crawling - Lack of monitoring and alerting, non convenient logging system - Overpriced Crawlera

description: Started using it 2 years ago, learning curve totally rely on the framework because the cloud platform is pretty intuitive, self explanatory and easy to use. Whenever I needed support I have been response fast and with a favorable solution.
author: Carlos B.
pros: A full integrated platform for a framework well done for it purpose. Easy to use, based on others frameworks structure, so if you are used to web development in python then it's a piece of cake to create spiders. The integration (scrapy + scrapinghub) its really good, from a simple deployment through a library or a docker makes it suitable for any need. Good support and constant improvement on the platform. A lot of plugins and, open to any feature needed.
cons: So far there is nothing I dislike about it.

review_title: Still leading on a more competitive space
description: It just works, except on cloudflare protected sites, you will need to add more expensive services or tricks. Tech support is very helpful and keep improving with new services.
author: Alfredo J.
pros: Their cloud proxy service, combined with scrapy is still a just-works, with ip rotation from different geolocations.
cons: Their other offers are expensive, even with the increasing competition. It needs a lot of work for many sites using cloud flare protection.

description: Coming from a "legacy" environment where everything was build from scratch I have to say that my experience with Scrapinghub is really positive. I started using some more advanced features such as ItemLoaders, Middleware etc.
author: Stefano F.
pros: Scrapinghub is so easy to use. With few setups you're ready to build your first spider. The integration with github and other addons (such as crawlera) makes things even easier to manage code deploy and proxy network management. Support for Python 3 is also a great improvement
cons: I'm still in early days with scrapinghub and at the moment did not see any big issues to take into account. One things that comes in my mind to be improved is the documentation.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Overall had a great experience using zyte.
author: Akshay A.
pros: Able to deploy my scrapers and also to debug using logs it's very helpful.
cons: It's pretty confusing sometimes while scheduling jobs as the timezone is in UTC. Even debugging logs gets harder.

description: Good, they are responsive and professional. Would recommend.
author: Tim R.
pros: As a business once you hit a certain size it makes sense outsourcing webscraping. Scrapinghub has the required specialism to do a good job.
cons: Still needs involved work from the client side to ensure data quality and consistency.

author: Madalina P.
pros: It is easy to use and the quality of the data is good
cons: It could be a little faster but for our needs it is a good product
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Great customer support and account managers.
author: Nitin S.
pros: Ease of use to download and manage various crawlers
cons: Too much data that requires a lot of cleanup activities. It would be great if the data can be cleaned and presented in ready to use form
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Never had a problem, and customer services always replied to our requests promptly, and with attention to detail.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We are very happy with Crawlera. It works like a charm with our Scrapy projects, just adding a few lines of code we completely forgot about proxies that ended up failing anyway.
cons: Some requests end up being very slow, but good news is most of them end up going through.

description: Scrapinghub has enabled us to streamline the scraping process for our company and sets the foundation for future scaling. I like the fact that scrapyhub handles the "Ops" part of scraping like provisioning cloud resources, staging data, and rotating IP addresses.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Scrapy is amazing, python api is easy to use, job dashboard is easy to understand, good documentation and code.
cons: Dynamic pages sometimes hard to scrape, nothing wrong with lua (for splash) as a language, but can be minor barrier to entry for python/javascript users.

description: I am very happy with Scrapinghub. Whenever I need to run a small scraper that I can't run on my laptop (since I don't leave it turned on 24/7), I just run it on Scrapy Cloud. Meanwhile, Crawlera is just the best!
author: Mikko G.
pros: I have been using Scrapy and Scrapinghub's services since 2013 and I'm so far very satisfied with their services. Crawlera, their proxy service, works very well! I don't have to setup a proxy farm anymore or configure my scrapers to point to thousands of proxy services as they do all the grunt work for you (it's all automated).
cons: Minor hiccups from time to time on the dashboard. It only affects me when I want to see historical stats but this problem doesn't affect functionality.

review_title: Scrapinghub allows me to do things other proxy servers don't do.
description: Scrapinghub gives my business higher quality raw data so my clients get better results.
author: Chris B.
pros: This software allows me to access 97% of the web resources I need. Other proxy servers I have used gave me 75% maximum.
cons: That its a time based subscription. I would prefer being able to buy a certain number of requests and renew when those run out.

review_title: Great proxy and scraping utils with superb support
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I use the crawlera proxy system from Scarpinghub. It is very straightforward to use and results are great.
cons: No cons until now. Once in a while a server/IP gets banned but they have a big pool of IPs to serve you.
author_profession: Head of Software Development / Responsable de Desarrollo de Software

author: Abhinav V.
pros: Our team being in limited number, setting up multiple tools for data extraction was a pain. We contacted an agency which quoted us a huge bill and that's when we found the Zyte. Few days spent in dev, no pain of servers, proxy rotation or anything and we got our job done.
cons: We're happy with the product and do not find any issues yet.

review_title: They don't promise what they sell.
description: I bought a $ 349 Advanced plan to get 200 concurrent requests. In their free trial, the response times are very low - about a few seconds. After purchasing the plan instead the requests together applied a delay on purpose thus canceling the potential of concurrent requests. In fact, it's like doing about 5/6 per second. After contacting their technical support, after a week, they tell me that an upgrade is required by paying an additional cost. Obviously I requested a refund since they did not promise what was advertised and told me that it was not possible since I had used the service. Of course I had used it, otherwise how could I tell that concurrent requests weren't working properly? I absolutely do not recommend this service for making me waste about a week to find a "fake" problem they know well and for asking me for an upgrade without even offering me a courtesy refund to apologize.
author: Gennaro M.
pros: Ease of installation and integration with API
cons: They don't respect what they communicate in the service.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I use the Python scrapy module to write crawlers to monitor competitors prices. The integration with Github makes it real easy to deploy code to the hub. The dashboards are very useful to monitor progress and schedule jobs. Really happy with this offering.
cons: In the beginning it was somewhat cumbersome to automate data extraction. Once I started using Python scrapinghub module life became easy. So also a pro!

description: From what I've used (basic spiders) it has been great, the page is really easy to use and the cli makes deploying easy.
author: Diego E.
pros: Open source, well documented and very efficient.
cons: Splash needs to be migrated to another tool IMO. I don't find LUA easy to work with as a developer; not because of the language but debugging and maintaining code is really hard. If you use scrapy-splash there is a black box that cannot interact with. I think going mainstream with javascript or python whilst unifying browser scrapping and scrapy itself would be an amazing decision. So, overall I would love to see a merge between scrapy and a browser scraper.

review_title: Worst thing I have ever used in my Software Engineering career
author: Umair A.
pros: No pros - its a library full of bugs
cons: *** NEVER USE THIS CRAP LIBRARY *** We had a paid plan, and this Splash is full of bugs Proxies for HTTPS urls do not work at all, the splash page just hangs forever There is no real-time UI that you can look whats happening inside Splash We created few tickets about the errors we were getting, they respond after 10+ days for a single ticket When taking screenshots, it generates white blank screenshots even the page is loaded perfectly When clicking on elements, it sometimes works, but sometimes just makes that element 'active' but does not click When selecting an element, it sometimes finds element, but sometimes does not, even though the source-code of page has that element when I print that in logs
